assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md explicitly states: 'Vulnerable library: openssl-libs (system RPM package)' and 'Ecosystem: **RPM**' and 'Lock file: `rpms.lock.yaml` (configured in Ecosystem Mappings for both streams)'. The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM, not Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a 2.2.x stream table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), and 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). The dependency chain section confirms 'openssl-libs found in rpms.lock.yaml at all build tags' as the data source.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md dependency chain states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present (openssl-libs found in rpms.lock.yaml at all build tags) -> explicit install' and 'The openssl-libs package is present in `rpms.lock.yaml` at every build tag in the 2.2.x stream, confirming it is an explicitly installed package (not inherited from the base image).' The lock file presence is explicitly used as the primary classification signal, with SBOM verification noted as skipped.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states: 'A single remediation task is required (RPM ecosystem -- Konflux release repo fix only, no upstream backport needed).' Only one jira.create_issue proposal is present, targeting repository 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (the Konflux release repo). No upstream backport task is proposed.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (CVE details and affected versions), '## Implementation Notes' (4 bullet points for update steps), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (3 checkbox items). No '## Files to Modify' section is present, which is correct per the assertion that it is intentionally omitted.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md dependency chain includes: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' Further elaborated: 'Note: SBOM verification via cosign was not performed because cosign is not available in this environment. The rpms.lock.yaml classification (explicit install) is used as the sole source of truth for package origin.'
